parallel_group.prompt:
Implement the library core for the kbu-beril-worktree PRD (read agent-io/prds/kbu-beril-worktree/fullprompt.md in the KBUtilLib repo in full, including the Implementation Decisions 'Confront round 1 resolutions' block and the Acceptance Criteria). Create a new package src/kbutillib/beril_worktree/ with two modules and NOTHING ELSE in this task (no CLI wiring, no launch proxy):

1. config.py — resolve and persist configuration in kbu's OWN config (~/.kbutillib/config.yaml via kbutillib.shared_env_utils.SharedEnvUtils; study how that module reads/saves YAML). Provide functions to resolve beril_root and worktree_root with precedence: explicit arg > env (BERIL_ROOT / WORKING_BERIL_DIRECTORY) > config (beril.root / beril.worktree_root) > for worktree_root only, default <beril_root>/../WorkingBERIL; beril_root has NO silent default and must raise a clear error if unresolved (AC #20). Provide a set_root helper that expands ~, resolves to absolute paths, and creates ~/.kbutillib/config.yaml and its parent dir if missing before writing beril.root and/or beril.worktree_root (AC #2, #19). Never write BERIL's ~/.config/beril/config.toml.

2. manager.py — class BerilWorktree(beril_root: Path, worktree_root: Path) with the interface: new(project_id, *, from_branch='main', open_cursor=False) -> Path; remove(project_id, *, force=False) -> bool; list() -> list of worktree info (live worktrees + reopenable projects/* branches); open(project_id) -> Path. Behavior, per the PRD and ACs: worktree dir is exactly <worktree_root>/<id>, branch is exactly projects/<id> (AC #4); validate <id> against [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ and reject anything else with a clear error and no side effects (AC #5); new creates branch projects/<id> off main when absent, adopts the existing branch (no -b) when present (AC #6); new creates .env and .venv-berdl symlinks pointing at <beril_root>/.env and <beril_root>/.venv-berdl, warning and continuing non-fatally if a target is missing (AC #7); new writes <worktree_root>/<id>.code-workspace (OUTSIDE the git tree) with a single folder {"name":"BERIL: <id>","path":"./<id>"}, copying the settings and extensions top-level keys from <beril_root>/BERIL.code-workspace when present else empty objects (AC #8); if <worktree_root>/<id> exists and is NOT a registered git worktree, new aborts and changes nothing (AC #9); open recreates the worktree from an existing projects/<id> branch if the dir is missing and errors (pointing to new) if the branch does not exist (AC #10); remove deletes ONLY the worktree directory via git worktree remove, NEVER git branch -d, and runs git worktree prune (AC #11); remove is idempotent — if the path is not registered in git worktree list it prints 'nothing to remove', deletes no files, returns False, exits cleanly (AC #12); remove refuses on uncommitted changes unless force=True (AC #13). ALL git operations run with git -C <beril_root> and never depend on cwd (AC #21).

Write a pytest suite (tests/ mirroring the repo's existing test layout) that exercises external behavior against a SCRATCH temporary git repo (git init, commit on main, seed projects/<id>/ dirs) — do not touch the real BERIL repo. Cover: new creates worktree+branch; new adopts an existing branch; remove deletes the dir and the branch still exists; remove idempotent no-op; remove dirty refusal + force override; symlink creation and that they are gitignored; workspace file is valid JSON, lives outside the worktree, folder points at ./<id>; ID validation rejects slashes; config resolution precedence; set_root persists to ~/.kbutillib/config.yaml (use a tmp HOME) and not to BERIL config.

IMPORTANT BUILD CONSTRAINT: KBUtilLib's pyproject.toml sets coverage fail_under = 100 — the test run FAILS unless coverage is 100%. Ensure tests cover every line, and mark genuinely untestable lines with '# pragma: no cover'. Match the repo's existing style, typing, and test conventions.
parallel_group.success_criteria: src/kbutillib/beril_worktree/{config.py,manager.py} exist; BerilWorktree exposes new/remove/list/open and config exposes the documented resolution + set_root; the new pytest suite passes against a scratch temp git repo and demonstrates: new creates worktree+projects/<id> branch, new adopts an existing branch, remove deletes only the directory while the branch persists, remove is an idempotent no-op when absent, remove refuses when dirty and honors force, .env/.venv-berdl symlinks are created and gitignored, the .code-workspace file is valid JSON outside the worktree pointing at ./<id>, ID validation rejects slashes, and config precedence (arg>env>config>default) plus set_root persistence to ~/.kbutillib/config.yaml all hold; overall coverage is 100% so the suite passes under fail_under=100.

parallel_group.prompt:
Build the CLI surface and launch proxy for the kbu-beril-worktree PRD on top of the already-merged library core (read agent-io/prds/kbu-beril-worktree/fullprompt.md in KBUtilLib in full, including Acceptance Criteria; the library package src/kbutillib/beril_worktree/{config.py,manager.py} already exists from the prior phase — reuse it, do not reimplement it).

1. Add a nested click group to src/kbutillib/cli/beril.py: a @click.group('worktree') registered via beril_cmd.add_command(worktree_cmd, name='worktree'), with subcommands new, open, start, rm, ls, set-root, doctor. Group-level options --beril-root PATH and --root PATH (alias --worktree-root) are available to every subcommand (AC #1); they resolve through config.py. set-root persists root(s) (AC #2). Commands delegate to BerilWorktree. new supports --open (launch Cursor). open and new --open run `cursor <worktree_root>/<id>.code-workspace` via subprocess; if cursor is not on PATH, print the path and a manual instruction rather than failing hard. ls prints a human-readable two-section listing by default and a stable JSON array [{"id","branch","path","live"}] sorted by id under --json (AC #14). Print the 'never run beril start inside a worktree' warning after a successful new, open, and start (AC #22).

2. Create src/kbutillib/beril_worktree/launch.py — the start proxy. kbu is pip-installed in the BERIL env, so import get_default_agent and get_vertex_config from beril_cli.config and _sync_auth_token from beril_cli.start. IMPORT, do NOT reimplement these. Replicate ONLY run_start's thin orchestration shell MINUS _checkout_release. Factor the pre-execvp work into a PURE function that returns (binary, argv, env_updates) so it is unit-testable without process replacement: resolve agent (arg or get_default_agent()), shutil.which the binary, for claude append --model opus when the user did not pass --model and set the initial prompt to /berdl_start when no prompt was passed and --skip-onboard is false (AC #15), and apply BERIL's EXACT Vertex env-key mapping from run_start (the specific keys: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLOUD_ML_REGION, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_PROJECT_ID, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, plus the Haiku model keys run_start sets) gated on get_vertex_config()['enabled'], only for claude — do NOT os.environ.update(get_vertex_config()), that dict is config not env vars (AC #16). The start command then chdir to the worktree, call the imported _sync_auth_token(worktree/.env), apply env, print the warning, and os.execvp the binary (mark the execvp line '# pragma: no cover'). start accepts --agent and --skip-onboard and forwards args after -- verbatim to the agent (AC #3).

3. doctor: assert beril_cli imports and that get_default_agent, get_vertex_config, _sync_auth_token all resolve; exit 0 on success, 1 if the import fails or any symbol is missing (naming the missing one), never 2+ (AC #17). Also report whether each configured worktree's .env/.venv-berdl symlink targets exist and are readable (AC #18).

Tests: CLI smoke (kbu beril worktree --help lists all 7 subcommands; each command wires to the library — use click's CliRunner and monkeypatch/stub BerilWorktree and subprocess so no real worktree/cursor is created); the import tripwire test asserting beril_cli + the 3 symbols import; the pure argv/env assembly function (opus default applied/suppressed, /berdl_start onboard injected and suppressed by --skip-onboard, no checkout ever, vertex keys applied only for claude when enabled); doctor matrix (beril_cli importable vs not, each symbol present vs missing — monkeypatch the import surface). Do NOT test os.execvp itself.

IMPORTANT BUILD CONSTRAINT: KBUtilLib's pyproject.toml sets coverage fail_under = 100 — the run FAILS below 100% coverage. Cover every line; mark only genuinely untestable lines (the execvp hand-off) with '# pragma: no cover'. Match the repo's existing click patterns (see the existing beril install/doctor commands), style, and typing.
parallel_group.success_criteria: `kbu beril worktree --help` lists new/open/start/rm/ls/set-root/doctor and the group accepts --beril-root/--root; the start proxy's pre-execvp assembly is a pure (binary, argv, env) function unit-tested to never include a release checkout, apply --model opus only when unspecified for claude, inject /berdl_start unless --skip-onboard or a prompt is passed, and apply BERIL's exact Vertex env keys only for claude when enabled; doctor exits 0 on success and 1 (naming the missing symbol) when beril_cli or any of get_default_agent/get_vertex_config/_sync_auth_token is unavailable, and reports .env/.venv-berdl symlink readability; an import-tripwire test asserts beril_cli + the three symbols resolve; CLI commands delegate to the library (verified via CliRunner with stubs, no real worktree/cursor created); the full pytest suite is green at 100% coverage.
